paper_title: Moving to the Adjacent Possible: Discovering Paths for Export Diversification in Rwanda
research_answer: The regional trade gap in chemicals represents the single largest opportunity among the identified frontier product categories. East African neighbors import $1.2 billion worth of chemical products, agrochemicals, and medicaments annually, while the region exports only $86 million of these goods — leaving a gap of over $1.1 billion. This dwarfs the gaps in other categories: machinery and electrical products show $888 million in regional imports versus just $38 million in regional exports, and construction materials show roughly $700 million in regional imports versus about $100 million in regional exports. Taken together, these three categories alone represent over $2.7 billion in regional import demand that is largely being met from outside the region, creating a substantial opportunity for any East African producer — including Rwanda — that can competitively supply these goods.
supporting_quotes: The regional imports of the 14 frontier products on this category amount to US$ 1.2 billion wile exports from the region are only US$ 86 million, representing a large opportunity for any regional producer who can competitively supply these products.
The 17 frontier products in this category account for US$888 million of regional imports and only US$38 million of exports from the region.
The region imports close to $700 million of these goods, and exports roughly US$ 100 million.
